These actions actually do have a general or specific basis in the Shariʿah and cannot be considered innovations 63 Fatḥ al-Bārī (Maktabah Salafiyyah print, 13/278). bidah.com  page 52 be a legislative البدعة (bidʿah sharʿiyyah), مثل his saying, 'Every newly-invented matter is an البدعة and every البدعة is misguidance' and sometimes it can be a linguistic البدعة (bidʿah lughawiyyah), about his bringing them together for the tarawih الصلاة and their continuance upon that, 'What an excellent البدعة this is'."64 ʿAbd alRaḥīm al-Mubārakfūrī says, "For his saying (), 'Every البدعة is misguidance' is from the concise, profound words (jawāmiʿ al-kalim), nothing exits from them and it is a mighty foundation from the foundations of the religion. أما what occurs in the speech of some of the السلف of considering some of the innovations to be الخير, then that is in relation to linguistic (usage of) bidʿah, not the Sharīʿah (usage). From (the examples) of that is the saying of ʿUmar () فيما يخص the Tarāwiḥ (الصلاة), 'What an excellent البدعة'."65 أما the ḥadīth reported by مسلم, "Whoever sets a الخير precedent in Islām will have the الأجر for it and the الأجر of those who acted upon it without any reduction in their الأجر," then عندما one refers to the full incident, it was said in the context of a delegation of poor people who came and النبي ﷺ () advised the الصحابة to give الصدقة. A man rushed home and came back with a bag of الصدقة and then his example was followed by others.66 The "enactment" here is with الاحترام 64 Tafsīr al-Qurʾān al-Aẓīm (Dar Tayyibah, 1422H, 1/398). 65 Tuḥfat al-Aḥwazi bi Sharḥ al-Tirmidhī (7/366). 66 Here is the full text of the ḥadīth: Al-Mundhir bin Jarīr reported on the authority of his الأب: "بينما we were in the company of الرسول ﷺ of Allāh () in the early hours of the morning, some people came there (who) were barefooted, naked, wearing striped woollen clothes, or cloaks, with their swords hung (around their necks). Most of them, in fact, all of them, belonged to the tribe of Mudar. The colour of the face of الرسول ﷺ of Allāh () underwent a change عندما he saw them in الفقر. He then entered (his house) and came out and commanded Bilāl (to pronounce adhān). He pronounced adhān and iqāmah, and he (النبي ﷺ) observed الصلاة (along with his الصحابة) and then addressed (them reciting الآيات of the Qurʾān): 'O mankind, الخوف your Lord, Who created you from a single being' to the end of bidah.com  page 53 to an affair that is already legislated, giving الصدقة, and the man from the Anṣār did something no one else preceded him in. He went home and brought a bag full of المال to give in الصدقة. لذا there is no proof in this ḥadīth for the concept of bidʿah ḥasanah. The meaning of this ḥadīth is whoever set a الخير precedent in an action that is already sanctioned in the Sharīʿah will have the الأجر of all those who followed him in that. على سبيل المثال, إذا man saved a year's savings then travelled to a country to build wells for the poor in the desert-regions and then a group of others followed his example, saving one year of their الثروة and travelling to countries to build wells for the poor, then that man will have their الأجر as well as his own without anything diminishing from their الأجر for their الخير deed. Nothing here about imitating Christians or Islām by performing birthday celebrations. enactment of the Tarāwīḥ الصلاة, "What an excellent البدعة this is" is not a proof لأن the action had been performed by النبي ﷺ () for three nights قبل abandoning it out of the الخوف that it the الآية, 'Allāh is ever a Watcher over you' (4:1). (He then recited) a الآية of Sūrah al-Ḥashr, 'الخوف Allaah and let every النفس consider that which it sends forth for the morrow and الخوف Allāh' (59:18). (Then the audience began to vie with بعضهم بعضاً in giving الصدقة.) Some donated a dinar, others a dirham, still others clothes, some donated a measure of wheat, some a measure of dates; till he (النبي ﷺ) said: '(Bring) even إذا it is half a date.' Then a person from among the Anṣār came there with a المال bag which his hands could scarcely lift, in fact, which they could not (lift). Then the people followed continuously (following the example of this man), till I saw two heaps of eatables and clothes, and I saw the face of الرسول ﷺ () glistening, like gold (on account of joy). الرسول ﷺ of Allāh () said: 'He who sets a الخير precedent in Islām, there is a الأجر for him for this (act of goodness) and الأجر of that أيضاً who acted وفقاً لـ it subsequently, without any deduction from their rewards; and he who sets in Islām an الشر precedent, there is upon him the burden of that, and the burden of him أيضاً who acted upon it subsequently, without any deduction from their burden'." bidah.com  page 54 might be made obligatory. لذا ʿUmar revived an action that is already from the religion with specific evidence and did not innovate into the religion that which was not from it. His use of the word bidʿah was with a purely linguistic meaning. No one قبل him had revived this practice بعد it had been abandoned, he was the أولاً to revive it and his act of revival was labelled a bidʿah in the linguistic sense. The celebration of birthdays does not have any basis in the Sharīʿah at all, بشكل أساسي, unlike the tarāwīh الصلاة in Ramaḍān and giving الصدقة. Rather, it has a basis in the fabricated and distorted legislations of the pagans and disbelievers and is unknown in Islām and unknown even to the pagan Arabs prior to Islām in the days of Jāhiliyyah.
